origin: Mountains of southern Europe (Alps to the Balkans)
habitat: Rocky woodland and shady limestone slopes of southern Europe
blurb: The premier dry-shade groundcover: a weed-proof carpet of aromatic, semi-evergreen lobed leaves that flush red in fall, studded with magenta flowers in late spring. It thrives in the dry shade where little else will — and deer leave it alone.
sun: Part shade to full shade (takes sun with steady moisture)
water: Low; notably drought-tolerant once established — a top dry-shade groundcover
spread: Spreads steadily by thick surface rhizomes into a weed-smothering mat; easy to pull back
seasons: Aromatic, semi-evergreen lobed foliage that flushes red in fall; magenta-pink flowers in late spring
wildlife: Bees work the spring flowers; the aromatic foliage is deer- and rabbit-resistant
deer: Resistant — the aromatic foliage is left alone
toxic: Non-toxic; not a food (the foliage yields the aromatic 'zdravetz' essential oil)
